title: Sharing one viewer across projects
question: How do viewer changes reach every project — copy per project, or share one template?
rationale: Each project links to one shared viewer instead of holding its own copy, so editing the template once updates every project automatically; only the decision data stays per-project. Links can break if a folder moves, but the generator repairs them on the next refresh.

options.label: One shared viewer; each project links to it (symlink)

tradeoffs.note: editing the template once shows up everywhere — no per-project copying
the viewer code is shared; just the decision files differ between projects
instant — projects read the same file, no rebuild needed
a link can break if a folder moves, but the generator self-heals it on the next refresh

options.label: Generator owns the viewer, overwrites each project's copy

tradeoffs.note: edit the template once; each project rewrites its copy
plain real files, no links to break
not instant — a project updates only on its next regenerate

options.label: One self-contained file rebuilt each time (inline CSS/JS)

tradeoffs.note: one output file, nothing to drift or go stale
not instant — a project updates only on its next regenerate

options.label: Each project keeps its own copy (the old d28 way)

tradeoffs.note: a project can hand-edit its own viewer
every update has to be copied into each project by hand — the problem we hit
